name: Hot Roll Bagels - Pillsbury Summer Fun Cook Book
recipeIngredient: Poppy seeds (or sesame seeds), 1 tablespoon (or 2 teaspoons)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the yeast from the foil packet with the flour mixture.
2. Stir in 1 cup of hot tap water, 1 egg, and margarine until the dough pulls cleanly away from the sides of the bowl.
3. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. With greased or floured hands, form the dough into a ball.
4. Knead the dough for 10 minutes until smooth. Cover with an inverted bowl and let rest for 5 minutes.
5. Divide the dough into 12 balls. With greased hands, shape each into a rope that is 8 inches long by 1 inch in diameter.
6. Dip one end of each rope in water and pinch the two ends together firmly.
7. Place the rings on a floured board and shape them into circles. Cover; let rise for 5 minutes.
8. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
9. Generously grease or line cookie sheets with parchment paper.
10. In a dutch oven or large saucepan, bring 2 quarts of water to boil. Reduce heat to simmer.
11. With a slotted spoon, drop the bagels one at a time into the boiling water for 5 seconds on each side.
12. Remove from water and place on cookie sheets. Brush with beaten egg and sprinkle with seeds.
13. Bake in the preheated oven for 22-28 minutes (or until golden brown), or until light golden brown if broiling briefly after 5 minutes of rising.
14. Remove from the oven and serve 12 bagels.
Nutritional Information:
1 bagel: 170 calories, 5g protein, 28g carbohydrates, 4g fat, 290mg sodium, 70mg potassium.
